channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Palo Alto Networks releases QRNG API framework
item.description: Cybersecurity company Palo Alto Networks has released the Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG) Open API framework, enabling users to prepare for quantum security threats.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to do when you can’t escape your old boss on social media
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: The OpenJDK’s plans for Java in 2025
item.description: Java enhancement plans for 2025 range from improving performance in the foreign function and memory (FFM) API, to working on ahead-of-time (AOT) code compilation, to finalizing the structured concurrency API, according to a recently published presentation from the OpenJDK community.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI opposes data deletion demand in India citing US legal constraints
item.description: OpenAI has informed the Delhi High Court that any directive requiring it to delete training data used for ChatGPT would conflict with its legal obligations under US law.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How employers can inspire software developers
item.description: Software developers are in demand. Those with extensive experience in this profession and the latest qualifications are in a position to choose the jobs they want. This makes it necessary for companies to position themselves as an attractive employer.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Power of Hybrid Cloud: Unlocking Real-Time Insights and Scalability
item.description: The hybrid cloud is a powerful computing environment that combines the best of both worlds, offering scalability, agility, and real-time insights. However, unlocking these benefits requires efficient data integration and management. In this article, we'll explore the challenges of traditional data integration methods and the key to success in achieving seamless data integration in a hybrid cloud solution.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek unveils open-source model to rival OpenAI o1
item.description: Chinese AI developer DeepSeek has unveiled an open-source version of its reasoning model, DeepSeek-R1, featuring 671 billion parameters and claiming performance superior to OpenAI’s o1 on key benchmarks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Skyrider X1: A Glimpse into a Future of Flying Motorcycles?
item.description: The Skyrider X1, an amphibious flying passenger motorcycle, promises to change how we think about personal mobility by combining land and air travel in one sleek design.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? This is probably why
item.description: The official POTUS and White House accounts are managed by the White House and “change when the occupant of the White House changes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Regeneron and auticon open doors for autistic IT professionals
item.description: Navigating the corporate world is challenging enough, but it can be especially difficult for autistic adults. According to the Autism Society, there are 5.4 million autistic adults in the US and among those with a college degree, up to 85% are either unemployed or underemployed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Taiwan's Green Iguana Problem: A Growing Concern
item.description: Taiwan faces a severe green iguana infestation, with up to 120,000 culls planned. Supporters urge humane methods for population control and agricultural sector protection.
item.dc:creator: Tai-Jing Wu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Instagram courts TikTok creators with cash bonuses up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is not only battling for survival this week but also facing fierce competition from rivals eager to poach its top influencers. Among them is Meta’s Instagram, reportedly offering cash bonuses to lure big TikTok creators to its platform, Reels.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US GPU export limits could bring cold war to AI, data center markets
item.description: The US Biden administration, during its final week in office, issued a rule limiting sales of powerful AI chips to more than 100 countries, to keep the technology out of the hands of Chinese companies and the military there.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: List: Tech moguls at Trump's inauguration
item.description: The heads of five of the top eight largest companies (by market cap) were seen at Trump's inauguration on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation
